Box.description:
Axis-aligned bounding box in normalized page coordinates.

Every value is a fraction of the page's width (`xmin`/`xmax`) or height
(`ymin`/`ymax`) in `[0, 1]`, with at most 8 decimal places. To convert to
pixels, multiply by the dimensions of whatever raster of the page you are
drawing on. Coordinates are clamped and rounded at construction so the
in-process value always equals the serialized one.

xmax.description: Right edge as a fraction of the page width, in `[0, 1]`.
xmax.title: Xmax

xmin.description: Left edge as a fraction of the page width, in `[0, 1]`.
xmin.title: Xmin

ymax.description: Bottom edge as a fraction of the page height, in `[0, 1]`.
ymax.title: Ymax

ymin.description: Top edge as a fraction of the page height, in `[0, 1]`.
ymin.title: Ymin

Box.title: Box

children.description: The pages of the document, in source order.
children.title: Children

markdown.description: The full document markdown — identical to the top-level `markdown` field, included so the structure tree is self-contained. Present only when `options.inline_markdown` is `true`.
markdown.title: Markdown

type.description: The node type. Identifies this node as the root of the structure tree.
type.title: Type

Element.description:
Hierarchical document element. All non-page elements share this shape.

Keys off `type`; optional fields are excluded from the serialized JSON
via ``exclude_none=True`` when not set.

atomic_grounding.description: Fine-grained grounding segments at the model's current granularity (visual lines today; finer in future versions, same schema). Present only on leaf elements — every type except `table`. `[]` only when segments are structurally impossible: `table_cell` (a cell has no finer granularity than itself) and elements whose markdown is suppressed via `blocks.<type>.markdown=false`. Any other leaf the model could not segment finer carries a single entry covering the element's full range and box. Omitted entirely when `options.atomic_grounding` is `false`.
atomic_grounding.title: Atomic Grounding

children.description: The cells (`table_cell` elements) of a `table` element. Present only when `type` is `table`.
children.title: Children

col.description: 0-indexed column position of this cell within its parent `table`. Present only on `table_cell` elements.
col.title: Col

colspan.description: Number of columns this cell spans. `1` for unmerged cells. Present only on `table_cell` elements.
colspan.title: Colspan

grounding.description: The element's spatial data: the page it appears on, its `[start, end)` range in the top-level `markdown` string, and its bounding box in normalized page coordinates.

id.description: Semantic element id, unique within the document. Format `<type>-<index>`, where `<index>` is a per-type 0-based counter assigned in reading order — `text-0` is the first text element in the document, `figure-0` the first figure, `table_cell-0` the first cell of the first table. Stable within a response but not across re-parses of the same document.
id.title: Id

markdown.description: The element's slice of the top-level `markdown` string (`markdown[grounding.range.start:grounding.range.end]`). `""` for zero-length ranges (e.g. blocks suppressed via `blocks.<type>.markdown=false`). Present only when `options.inline_markdown` is `true`.
markdown.title: Markdown

row.description: 0-indexed row position of this cell within its parent `table`. Present only on `table_cell` elements.
row.title: Row

rowspan.description: Number of rows this cell spans. `1` for unmerged cells. Present only on `table_cell` elements.
rowspan.title: Rowspan

type.description: The element type. Determines which optional fields appear on this element.
type.title: Type

Element.title: Element

Grounding.description:
Where a node lives: its page, its slice of `markdown`, and its box.

The same shape is used for page nodes, element nodes, and each
`atomic_grounding` entry, so any grounding object is self-contained — it
can be lifted out of the tree and still locates its content.

box.description: Bounding box in normalized page coordinates (`0`–`1` fractions of page width/height, at most 8 decimal places). A page node's box is always the full page `{0, 0, 1, 1}`.

page.description: 1-indexed page number this grounding is on. On a page node, the page's own number.
page.title: Page

range.description: `[start, end)` offsets into the top-level `markdown` string covered by this node or segment.

Grounding.title: Grounding

children.description: The elements detected on this page, in reading order. Empty for failed pages.
children.title: Children

grounding.description: The page's spatial data: `page` is the 1-indexed page number in the source document (not contiguous when `options.pages` filters out some pages); `range` covers this page's content in the top-level `markdown` string (zero-length `start == end` for failed pages); `box` is always the full page `{0, 0, 1, 1}`.

markdown.description: This page's slice of the top-level `markdown` string (`markdown[grounding.range.start:grounding.range.end]`). `""` for failed pages. Present only when `options.inline_markdown` is `true`.
markdown.title: Markdown

reason.description: Failure reason. Present only when `status` is `failed`.
reason.title: Reason

status.description: Whether this page was parsed successfully (`ok`) or failed (`failed`).
status.title: Status

type.description: The node type. Identifies this node as a page in the structure tree.
type.title: Type

ParseBilling.description: Billing summary for one parse request.

service_tier.description: The service tier the request ran in: `standard` or `priority`. A sync request reports `priority` (same lane, same price).
service_tier.title: Service Tier

ParseBilling.title: ParseBilling

ParseMetadata.description:
Information about a parse request. This is the customer-facing shape —
the internal billing-canonical metadata (``aide_common.parse_schema.ParseMetadata``)
is projected into it at the render boundary (``project_customer_metadata``).

billing.description: Billing summary: the service tier and the credits charged.

failed_pages.description: 1-indexed page numbers that failed to parse. Empty when all pages succeed; failed pages also appear in `structure.children` with `status: failed`.
failed_pages.title: Failed Pages

job_id.description: The parse job identifier — always server-minted and unique per submit. On the async `/jobs` route this is the id the caller polls. Correlates with the job's entry in your billing dashboard. Format: ``<service>-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` matching ``^(parse|extract)-[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}$``. Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.
job_id.title: Job Id

model_version.description: The exact model snapshot that parsed the document, e.g. `dpt-3-pro-20260710`.
model_version.title: Model Version

openapi_spec.description: URL of the OpenAPI spec covering this API, for inspection and client generation.
openapi_spec.title: Openapi Spec

output_markdown_chars.description: Number of Unicode code points in the returned `markdown` string.
output_markdown_chars.title: Output Markdown Chars

page_count.description: Total number of pages in the source document. Includes pages filtered out by `options.pages`; the actual returned pages are in `structure.children`.
page_count.title: Page Count

range_units.description: Units of every `range` offset in the response. Always `"unicode_codepoints"` (Unicode code points into `markdown`). Declared explicitly so consumers know how to slice the string — e.g. JavaScript strings are UTF-16, so a naive `.slice()` drifts when the markdown contains astral characters; such consumers must convert code-point offsets to UTF-16 indices first.
range_units.title: Range Units

ParseMetadata.title: ParseMetadata

ParseResponse.description:
The parse result: the full document as `markdown`, its hierarchical
`structure` (with per-node spatial `grounding` inline), and request
`metadata`.

markdown.description: The full document as a single Markdown string, in reading order.
markdown.title: Markdown

metadata.description: Information about the request: model version, page count, duration, billing, and more.

structure.description: The document's hierarchical structure: pages and the elements detected on each page. Every node below the root carries its spatial data inline in a `grounding` object (`{page, range, box}`, normalized page coordinates); leaf elements additionally carry `atomic_grounding`.

ParseResponse.title: ParseResponse

PrebuiltWorkflowStep.description:
A Phase 1 step: references a server-defined pipeline by name.

The ``name`` identifies the prebuilt pipeline to run AND becomes the key
under which this step's result appears in the response ``output`` map.
The ``document`` field is a reference into the ``inputs`` namespace
(``"$inputs.<logical-name>"``).

document.description: Reference into the ``inputs`` namespace, e.g. ``"$inputs.report"``. The referenced input must be declared in the ``inputs`` form field.
document.title: Document

name.description: Workflow to run — doubles as the result key in ``output``. v1 only: ``"parse-extract"``.
name.title: Name

options.description: Parse + extract options for this step. Omit for defaults.

schema.description: JSON Schema for fields to extract.
schema.title: Schema

PrebuiltWorkflowStep.title: PrebuiltWorkflowStep

Range.description: A `[start, end)` slice of the top-level `markdown` string.

end.description: Exclusive end offset into the top-level `markdown` string, in the units declared by `metadata.range_units` (Unicode code points).
end.title: End

start.description: Inclusive start offset into the top-level `markdown` string, in the units declared by `metadata.range_units` (Unicode code points).
start.title: Start

Range.title: Range

V2Billing.description:
Billing summary: the service tier the request ran in and the credits
charged.

service_tier.description: The service tier the request ran in: `standard` or `priority`. A sync request reports `priority` (same lane, same price).
service_tier.title: Service Tier

V2Billing.title: V2Billing

V2ExtractMetadata.description: Response metadata for a v2 extract call.

billing.description: Billing summary: the service tier the request ran in and the credits charged.

doc_id.description: Present when the input markdown contained a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment (embedded by ``POST /v2/parse``). Links this extract call to the originating parse job.
doc_id.title: Doc Id

input_markdown_chars.description: Characters (Unicode code points) in the input markdown as submitted — the input basis of the credit charge.
input_markdown_chars.title: Input Markdown Chars

job_id.description: Gateway job id (workflow id). Matches the ``x-request-id`` the gateway minted for this request and the billing row id in vision-agent.
job_id.title: Job Id

openapi_spec.description: URL of the OpenAPI spec covering this API, for inspection and client generation.

output_extraction_chars.description: Characters in the serialized extraction output — the output basis of the credit charge.
output_extraction_chars.title: Output Extraction Chars

range_units.description: Units of every `range` offset in the response. Always `"unicode_codepoints"` (Unicode code points into `markdown`). Declared explicitly so consumers know how to slice the string — e.g. JavaScript strings are UTF-16, so a naive `.slice()` drifts when the markdown contains astral characters.
range_units.title: Range Units

V2ExtractMetadata.title: V2ExtractMetadata

strict.description: When ``true``, a schema containing fields the model cannot extract fails with a validation error — HTTP 422 on the sync route, or a failed job (``status: "failed"``) on the async ``/jobs`` route. When ``false`` (default), unsupported fields are skipped and extraction continues.
strict.title: Strict

V2GroundMetadata.description: Response metadata for a v2 ground call.

billing.description: Billing summary: the service tier the request ran in and the credits charged.

job_id.description: Gateway job id (workflow id). Matches the ``x-request-id`` the gateway minted for this request.
job_id.title: Job Id

openapi_spec.description: URL of the OpenAPI spec covering this API, for inspection and client generation.

V2GroundMetadata.title: V2GroundMetadata

V2WorkflowMetadata.description:
Response metadata for a v2 workflow call
(``docs/pipeline-v2-proposal.md`` → Top-level metadata).

billing.description: Billing summary: the service tier the request ran in and the credits charged.

duration_ms.description: Total end-to-end duration across all stages in milliseconds.
duration_ms.title: Duration Ms

openapi_spec.description: URL of the OpenAPI spec covering this API, for inspection and client generation.

V2WorkflowMetadata.title: V2WorkflowMetadata

WorkflowDocumentInput.description:
One declared document input in the ``inputs`` map.

A caller supplies exactly one of ``document`` or ``document_url``:

* ``document`` — name of a multipart form part carrying the raw bytes. The
  gateway stages those bytes internally before the workflow runs.
* ``document_url`` — public URL (SSRF-validated at submit; fetched and staged
  by the gateway before the workflow starts).

document.description: Name of the multipart form part carrying the document bytes. The gateway resolves this to an internal ref before validation; it is not stored in the final request object.
document.title: Document

document_url.description: URL to fetch the document from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.
document_url.title: Document Url

WorkflowDocumentInput.title: WorkflowDocumentInput

WorkflowStepOptions.description:
Unified options for the ``parse-extract`` prebuilt step.

``pages`` applies to the parse stage; ``strict`` applies to the extract
stage. Omit entirely to use each stage's defaults.

pages.description: 1-indexed page numbers to process. ``null`` = all pages. Values < 1 are rejected; numbers > page_count are silently ignored.
pages.title: Pages

strict.description: When ``true``, a schema containing fields the model cannot extract fails with a validation error — HTTP 422 on the sync route, or a failed job on the async ``/jobs`` route. When ``false``, unsupported fields are skipped.
strict.title: Strict

WorkflowStepOptions.title: WorkflowStepOptions

info.description: AIDE Gateway — proxy surface + Temporal job surface.
info.title: AIDE Gateway

post.description:
Stage bytes on the data plane; pass the returned ``file_ref`` in
job inputs (the contract request types take ``*_ref`` fields).

schema.title: Response Stage File V1 Files Post

post.description: Extract structured data from a Markdown document according to a JSON schema, with character-span grounding into the source Markdown. Runs synchronously and returns the result inline.

schema.description:
Input to V2ExtractOperationWorkflow.

Provide the markdown as an inline ``markdown`` string, as a multipart file
part named ``markdown`` (for large inputs — the gateway stages the upload
internally), or via a public ``markdown_url``. Exactly one source must be
supplied.

markdown.description: Markdown string to extract from, or a multipart FILE part carrying the markdown (large inputs — uploads are staged by the gateway). Can come from any source — LandingAI parse output, a third-party parser, or hand-authored text. When the markdown was produced by ``POST /v2/parse``, it ends with a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment that the service reads automatically and echoes as ``metadata.doc_id``.
markdown.title: Markdown

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for extraction. Use ``extract-latest`` to use the latest version.
model.title: Model

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the fields to extract. The schema must be an object type with a ``properties`` map of field names to their types and descriptions.
schema.title: Schema
V2ExtractRequest

anyOf.description: Markdown string to extract from, or a multipart FILE part carrying the markdown (large inputs — uploads are staged by the gateway). Can come from any source — LandingAI parse output, a third-party parser, or hand-authored text. When the markdown was produced by ``POST /v2/parse``, it ends with a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment that the service reads automatically and echoes as ``metadata.doc_id``.

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time. JSON-serialized string in form data.
markdown_url.title: Markdown Url

model.description: The version of the model to use for extraction. Use ``extract-latest`` to use the latest version. JSON-serialized string in form data.
model.title: Model

options.description: Extraction options (``strict``). Omit for defaults. JSON-serialized string in form data.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the fields to extract. The schema must be an object type with a ``properties`` map of field names to their types and descriptions. JSON-serialized string in form data.
schema.title: Schema

schema.description:
Result returned by V2ExtractOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/extract``
response body (``docs/extract-v2-proposal.md`` → Response).

``extraction`` and ``extraction_metadata`` mirror each other structurally:
leaf values in ``extraction`` are replaced by ``ExtractionFieldMetadata``
objects in ``extraction_metadata``.

extraction.description: Extracted values conforming to the request ``schema``.
extraction.title: Extraction

extraction_metadata.description: Per-field metadata, mirroring ``extraction`` with leaf values replaced by ``{value, ranges}`` objects.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

metadata.description: Request metadata (job_id, model_version, duration_ms, doc_id, billing).

schema_violation_error.description: Set when ``options.strict`` is false and the schema contained fields the model could not extract — the extraction is partial.
schema_violation_error.title: Schema Violation Error

schema.title: V2ExtractResult

get.description: List your Extract jobs, newest first.

schema.title: Page

schema.description: Filter by job status.
schema.title: Status

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this v2-extract job. Format: ``extract-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` (``[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}`` tail). Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

post.description: Extract structured data from a Markdown document according to a JSON schema, with character-span grounding into the source Markdown. Runs asynchronously and returns a job ID; use it to poll for status and retrieve the result once processing completes.

schema.description:
Input to V2ExtractOperationWorkflow.

Provide the markdown as an inline ``markdown`` string, as a multipart file
part named ``markdown`` (for large inputs — the gateway stages the upload
internally), or via a public ``markdown_url``. Exactly one source must be
supplied.

markdown.description: Markdown string to extract from, or a multipart FILE part carrying the markdown (large inputs — uploads are staged by the gateway). Can come from any source — LandingAI parse output, a third-party parser, or hand-authored text. When the markdown was produced by ``POST /v2/parse``, it ends with a ``<!-- doc_id=<id> -->`` comment that the service reads automatically and echoes as ``metadata.doc_id``.

markdown_url.description: URL to fetch the markdown from. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.

model.description: The version of the model to use for extraction. Use ``extract-latest`` to use the latest version.

output_save_url.description: URL to save the result to — e.g. a presigned S3 PUT URL. Async jobs only. When set, the finished result is delivered (HTTP PUT) to this URL and the completed job reports ``output_url`` instead of an inline ``result``. Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time.

schema.description: JSON Schema describing the fields to extract. The schema must be an object type with a ``properties`` map of field names to their types and descriptions.

service_tier.description: Async service tier. ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate; absent → ``standard``.

Must be a public http(s) URL; private/loopback IPs are rejected at submit time. JSON-serialized string in form data.

service_tier.description: Async service tier. ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate; absent → ``standard``.

Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

get.description: Get the status of an async Extract job, including its result once the job has completed.

parameters.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
schema.title: Job Id

completed_at.description: Present once the job is terminal.

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this v2-extract job. Format: ``extract-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` (``[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}`` tail). Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

output_url.description: The URL the result was delivered to. Present once the job has ``completed`` and ``output_save_url`` was set, instead of inline ``result``.

progress.description: Job completion as a decimal from 0 (not started) to 1 (complete). Present while ``processing``.

anyOf.description:
Result returned by V2ExtractOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/extract``
response body (``docs/extract-v2-proposal.md`` → Response).

``extraction`` and ``extraction_metadata`` mirror each other structurally:
leaf values in ``extraction`` are replaced by ``ExtractionFieldMetadata``
objects in ``extraction_metadata``.

extraction.description: Extracted values conforming to the request ``schema``.
extraction.title: Extraction

extraction_metadata.description: Per-field metadata, mirroring ``extraction`` with leaf values replaced by ``{value, ranges}`` objects.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

metadata.description: Request metadata (job_id, model_version, duration_ms, doc_id, billing).

schema_violation_error.description: Set when ``options.strict`` is false and the schema contained fields the model could not extract — the extraction is partial.
schema_violation_error.title: Schema Violation Error

anyOf.title: V2ExtractResult

result.description: Present once status is ``completed`` and ``output_save_url`` was not set. When ``output_save_url`` was set, the result is delivered there and ``output_url`` is returned instead.

post.description: Map extracted fields to the document blocks they were quoted from. Takes the extraction_metadata from an extract call and the structure tree from the parse call the markdown came from, and returns, for every extracted field, the overlapping blocks with their ids, page numbers, and bounding boxes. Runs synchronously and returns the result inline.

schema.description:
Input to V2GroundOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/ground`` request body.

A pure, stateless join: each ``extraction_metadata`` leaf's ``ranges``
(char offsets into the markdown both artifacts were produced from) is
overlapped against the ``grounding.range`` carried on every ``structure``
block, and the matching blocks are returned. Nothing is stored server-side,
so block ids in the response resolve only against the ``structure`` tree
supplied here — pairing an extraction with the parse result it actually
came from is the caller's responsibility.

extraction_metadata.description: The ``extraction_metadata`` object returned by ``POST /v2/extract`` (or the pipeline's extract step): a tree mirroring your extraction schema whose leaves are ``{value, ranges}`` objects, where ``ranges`` are ``{start, end}`` Unicode code point offsets into the parse markdown.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

structure.description: The ``structure`` tree from the parse response the extraction was produced from. Every block in the tree carries its ``grounding`` (``{page, range, box}``) inline; block ids in the response resolve against this exact tree.
structure.title: Structure

schema.title: V2GroundRequest

extraction_metadata.description: The ``extraction_metadata`` object returned by ``POST /v2/extract`` (or the pipeline's extract step): a tree mirroring your extraction schema whose leaves are ``{value, ranges}`` objects, where ``ranges`` are ``{start, end}`` Unicode code point offsets into the parse markdown. JSON-serialized string in form data.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

structure.description: The ``structure`` tree from the parse response the extraction was produced from. Every block in the tree carries its ``grounding`` (``{page, range, box}``) inline; block ids in the response resolve against this exact tree. JSON-serialized string in form data.
structure.title: Structure

schema.description:
Result returned by V2GroundOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/ground``
response body.

``grounding`` MIRRORS the ``extraction_metadata`` tree: nested objects and
arrays keep their shape, and each ``{value, ranges}`` leaf is replaced by
the list of structure blocks its ranges overlap (the block-hit shape
documented on the field). It is NOT a flat map — a nested schema field like
``issuer.name`` resolves to ``grounding["issuer"]["name"]``.

grounding.description: A tree mirroring ``extraction_metadata``: nested objects and arrays keep their shape, and each ``{value, ranges}`` leaf is replaced by the list of blocks its ranges overlap, in reading order. Each entry carries ``block_id`` and ``type`` identifying the matched ``structure`` block, ``parent_id`` naming the enclosing block for nested blocks (table cells), and the block's own ``grounding`` object (``{page, range, box}``) verbatim. When the block itself carries ``atomic_grounding``, the entry also lists the overlapping subset as ``{index, page, range, box}`` objects, where ``index`` is the position in the block's own ``atomic_grounding`` array; ``[]`` means the block matched but no individual entry did, and the key is omitted for blocks that carry no ``atomic_grounding``. A leaf is ``null`` when its ``ranges`` was ``null`` (a synthesised value, with no supporting passage to look up) and ``[]`` when valid ranges overlapped no block (which usually indicates a mismatched extraction/structure pair).
grounding.title: Grounding

schema.title: V2GroundResult

Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

post.description: Map extracted fields to the document blocks they were quoted from. Takes the extraction_metadata from an extract call and the structure tree from the parse call the markdown came from, and returns, for every extracted field, the overlapping blocks with their ids, page numbers, and bounding boxes. Runs asynchronously and returns a job ID; use it to poll for status and retrieve the result once processing completes.

schema.description:
Input to V2GroundOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/ground`` request body.

A pure, stateless join: each ``extraction_metadata`` leaf's ``ranges``
(char offsets into the markdown both artifacts were produced from) is
overlapped against the ``grounding.range`` carried on every ``structure``
block, and the matching blocks are returned. Nothing is stored server-side,
so block ids in the response resolve only against the ``structure`` tree
supplied here — pairing an extraction with the parse result it actually
came from is the caller's responsibility.

extraction_metadata.description: The ``extraction_metadata`` object returned by ``POST /v2/extract`` (or the pipeline's extract step): a tree mirroring your extraction schema whose leaves are ``{value, ranges}`` objects, where ``ranges`` are ``{start, end}`` Unicode code point offsets into the parse markdown.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

structure.description: The ``structure`` tree from the parse response the extraction was produced from. Every block in the tree carries its ``grounding`` (``{page, range, box}``) inline; block ids in the response resolve against this exact tree.
structure.title: Structure

schema.title: V2GroundRequest

extraction_metadata.description: The ``extraction_metadata`` object returned by ``POST /v2/extract`` (or the pipeline's extract step): a tree mirroring your extraction schema whose leaves are ``{value, ranges}`` objects, where ``ranges`` are ``{start, end}`` Unicode code point offsets into the parse markdown. JSON-serialized string in form data.
extraction_metadata.title: Extraction Metadata

structure.description: The ``structure`` tree from the parse response the extraction was produced from. Every block in the tree carries its ``grounding`` (``{page, range, box}``) inline; block ids in the response resolve against this exact tree. JSON-serialized string in form data.
structure.title: Structure

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this v2-ground job. Format: ``ground-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` (``[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}`` tail). Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

get.description: Get the status of an async Ground job, including its result once the job has completed.

parameters.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
schema.title: Job Id

completed_at.description: Present once the job is terminal.

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this v2-ground job. Format: ``ground-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` (``[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}`` tail). Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

progress.description: Job completion as a decimal from 0 (not started) to 1 (complete). Present while ``processing``.

anyOf.description:
Result returned by V2GroundOperationWorkflow — the ``/v2/ground``
response body.

``grounding`` MIRRORS the ``extraction_metadata`` tree: nested objects and
arrays keep their shape, and each ``{value, ranges}`` leaf is replaced by
the list of structure blocks its ranges overlap (the block-hit shape
documented on the field). It is NOT a flat map — a nested schema field like
``issuer.name`` resolves to ``grounding["issuer"]["name"]``.

grounding.description: A tree mirroring ``extraction_metadata``: nested objects and arrays keep their shape, and each ``{value, ranges}`` leaf is replaced by the list of blocks its ranges overlap, in reading order. Each entry carries ``block_id`` and ``type`` identifying the matched ``structure`` block, ``parent_id`` naming the enclosing block for nested blocks (table cells), and the block's own ``grounding`` object (``{page, range, box}``) verbatim. When the block itself carries ``atomic_grounding``, the entry also lists the overlapping subset as ``{index, page, range, box}`` objects, where ``index`` is the position in the block's own ``atomic_grounding`` array; ``[]`` means the block matched but no individual entry did, and the key is omitted for blocks that carry no ``atomic_grounding``. A leaf is ``null`` when its ``ranges`` was ``null`` (a synthesised value, with no supporting passage to look up) and ``[]`` when valid ranges overlapped no block (which usually indicates a mismatched extraction/structure pair).
grounding.title: Grounding

anyOf.title: V2GroundResult

post.description: Parse a document and return the parse response inline.

document.description: The file to parse. The file must be a PDF or image; see the list of [supported file types](https://docs.landing.ai/dpt3/file-types). Provide either `document` or `document_url`, not both.

document_url.description: A publicly accessible URL to the file to parse. The file must be a PDF or image; see the list of [supported file types](https://docs.landing.ai/dpt3/file-types). Provide either `document` or `document_url`, not both.

model.description: The DPT-3 model snapshot to use for this request. Accepts a dated snapshot (for example, `dpt-3-pro-20260710`), the `dpt-3-pro-latest` alias, or the bare `dpt-3-pro` family name (equivalent to `dpt-3-pro-latest`). Defaults to the latest DPT-3 Pro snapshot.

options.description: Optional object that customizes the parse. Use it to select which pages to process, adjust how content appears in the Markdown, or control how much detail the response includes. Sent as a JSON-serialized string in form data.

atomic_grounding.description: Include the fine-grained `atomic_grounding` array on leaf elements. Set `false` to omit the field entirely from every node.
atomic_grounding.title: Atomic Grounding

inline_markdown.description: Include each node's slice of the document `markdown` inline as a `markdown` field on every structure node: the document root, each page, and each element (including table cells). `atomic_grounding` entries do not carry it.
inline_markdown.title: Inline Markdown

password.description: Password for encrypted PDFs. Not currently supported — providing a value returns a 422 error; decrypt the file before uploading.
password.title: Password

options.title: ParseOptions

206.description: Partial success (some pages failed to parse)

schema.description: Page number (0-indexed).
schema.title: Page

schema.description: Number of items per page.
schema.title: Page Size

schema.description: Filter by job status.
schema.title: Status

has_more.description: Whether more jobs exist beyond this page; request the next ``page`` to fetch them.

jobs.description: The caller's parse jobs for this page, newest first.

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the job finished, if terminal.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the job was created.

failure_reason.description: The reason the job failed. Present only when ``status`` is ``failed``.

job_id.description: The unique identifier for the parse job. Format: ``<service>-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` matching ``^(parse|extract)-[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}$``. Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

model_version.description: The model snapshot used to parse the document.

post.description: Parse a document asynchronously. Returns a job ID; use it to poll for the job's status and retrieve the parse result once processing completes.

document.description: The file to parse. The file must be a PDF or image; see the list of [supported file types](https://docs.landing.ai/dpt3/file-types). Provide either `document` or `document_url`, not both.

document_url.description: A publicly accessible URL to the file to parse. The file must be a PDF or image; see the list of [supported file types](https://docs.landing.ai/dpt3/file-types). Provide either `document` or `document_url`, not both.

model.description: The DPT-3 model snapshot to use for this request. Accepts a dated snapshot (for example, `dpt-3-pro-20260710`), the `dpt-3-pro-latest` alias, or the bare `dpt-3-pro` family name (equivalent to `dpt-3-pro-latest`). Defaults to the latest DPT-3 Pro snapshot.

options.description: Optional object that customizes the parse. Use it to select which pages to process, adjust how content appears in the Markdown, or control how much detail the response includes. Sent as a JSON-serialized string in form data.

atomic_grounding.description: Include the fine-grained `atomic_grounding` array on leaf elements. Set `false` to omit the field entirely from every node.
atomic_grounding.title: Atomic Grounding

inline_markdown.description: Include each node's slice of the document `markdown` inline as a `markdown` field on every structure node: the document root, each page, and each element (including table cells). `atomic_grounding` entries do not carry it.
inline_markdown.title: Inline Markdown

password.description: Password for encrypted PDFs. Not currently supported — providing a value returns a 422 error; decrypt the file before uploading.
password.title: Password

options.title: ParseOptions

output_save_url.description: Public URL the full response is delivered to; the API response then carries ``output_url`` instead of inline data.

service_tier.description: Async service tier (``POST /jobs`` only). ``priority`` runs in the fast lane at the sync billing rate; absent → ``standard``.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the job was created.

job_id.description: The unique identifier for the created parse job. Poll ``GET /v2/parse/jobs/{job_id}`` for its status and result. Format: ``<service>-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` matching ``^(parse|extract)-[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}$``. Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

status.description: The job's status at creation — normally ``pending`` (a just-created job that is still running is reported as ``pending``), but may already be a terminal ``completed`` / ``failed`` if the job finished before the create response was rendered.

get.description: Get the status of an async parse job. Once the job has ``completed``, ``result`` carries the parse response (or ``output_url`` when ``output_save_url`` was set). Partial success (some pages failed) is reported in ``result.metadata.failed_pages``.

parameters.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
schema.title: Job Id

completed_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp; present once the job is terminal.

created_at.description: ISO-8601 timestamp for when the job was created.

error.description: Present once the job has ``failed`` — the failure code + message.

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this parse job. Format: ``<service>-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` matching ``^(parse|extract)-[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}$``. Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

output_url.description: The URL the result was delivered to. Present once the job has ``completed`` and ``output_save_url`` was set, instead of inline ``result``.

progress.description: Job completion as a decimal from 0 (not started) to 1 (complete). Present while ``processing``.

result.description: The parse response, present once the job has ``completed`` and ``output_save_url`` was not set. When ``output_save_url`` was set, the result is delivered there and ``output_url`` is returned instead.

post.description:
Run synchronously and return the result inline.

Accepts ``application/json`` (the request fields as the body) or
``multipart/form-data`` (file fields are staged automatically).

Returns **504** if the work does not finish within the wait
window — long-running work belongs on the async ``POST
{path}/jobs`` route, which returns 202 immediately and is polled
via ``GET {path}/jobs/{job_id}``. On a 504 the workflow is
CANCELLED (a sync caller can never collect the result); a retry
starts the work over as a fresh job.

schema.description:
Input to V2WorkflowOperationWorkflow.

Phase 1 API design (``docs/pipeline-v2-proposal.md``):

``inputs`` declares named document sources. Each entry binds a logical name
to a binary upload (via ``document``, the multipart field name) or a URL
(via ``document_url``). The gateway stages uploads internally before the
workflow runs.

``steps`` is a one-element array containing a prebuilt step. Its ``document``
field references an entry from ``inputs`` (``"$inputs.<name>"``). The ``schema``
field carries the JSON Schema for the extract stage.

``output`` is an optional projection map. Each key maps to a ``$output.``
reference into the step results. Omitted → the full results tree is returned
under ``output``.

Example — minimal request (parse-extract, default options)::

    inputs = {"report": {"document": "report_pdf"}}
    steps  = [{"name": "parse-extract", "document": "$inputs.report",
               "schema": {"type": "object", "properties": {"revenue": {"type": "string"}}}}]
    report_pdf = [binary]

inputs.description: Named document sources. Each key is a logical name referenced from steps as ``"$inputs.<name>"``. The gateway stages binary multipart uploads internally before the workflow runs.
inputs.title: Inputs

output.description: Optional projection map. Keys are caller-chosen response field names; values are ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` references. Omitted → full results tree returned under ``output``.
output.title: Output

steps.description: Phase 1: a single prebuilt-pipeline step (name must be ``"parse-extract"``). Its result appears in ``output`` under the step's name.
steps.title: Steps

schema.title: V2WorkflowRequest

inputs.description: Named document sources. Each key is a logical name referenced from steps as ``"$inputs.<name>"``. The gateway stages binary multipart uploads internally before the workflow runs. JSON-serialized string in form data.
inputs.title: Inputs

output.description: Optional projection map. Keys are caller-chosen response field names; values are ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` references. Omitted → full results tree returned under ``output``. JSON-serialized string in form data.
output.title: Output

steps.description: Phase 1: a single prebuilt-pipeline step (name must be ``"parse-extract"``). Its result appears in ``output`` under the step's name. JSON-serialized string in form data.
steps.title: Steps

schema.description:
Result returned by V2WorkflowOperationWorkflow.

``output`` is keyed by step name (or by caller-chosen projection keys when
``output`` was provided in the request). For the ``"parse-extract"`` pipeline
with no projection, the keys are ``"parse-extract"`` → ``{parse, extract, ground}``:

* ``output["parse-extract"]["parse"]`` — full parse response (markdown,
  structure with per-node grounding inline, metadata).
* ``output["parse-extract"]["extract"]`` — extraction + ``extraction_metadata``
  with ``{value, ranges}`` leaves.
* ``output["parse-extract"]["ground"]`` — a tree MIRRORING
  ``extraction_metadata``: each ``{value, ranges}`` leaf is replaced by its
  resolved block-hit list (``[{block_id, type, parent_id?, grounding,
  atomic_grounding?}]`` — the same shape ``/v2/ground`` returns; see
  ``V2GroundResult.grounding`` for the full field-level contract), or
  ``null`` for a synthesised value. Nested objects and arrays keep their
  shape, so ``ground.issuer.name`` parallels
  ``extraction_metadata.issuer.name``. Resolved server-side — no
  client-side span lookup needed.

References into the results use the ``$output.<step>.<field>...`` grammar
(see ``output`` field of the request).

output.description: Step results keyed by step name, or a caller-defined projection. For ``parse-extract`` with no projection: ``{"parse-extract": {parse, extract, ground}}``.
output.title: Output

schema.title: V2WorkflowResult

schema.description: Page number (0-indexed).
schema.title: Page

schema.description: Number of items per page.
schema.title: Page Size

schema.description: Filter by job status.
schema.title: Status

job_id.description: The unique identifier for this v2-workflow job. Format: ``v2-workflow-<26-character Crockford base32 ULID>`` (``[0-9a-hjkmnp-tv-z]{26}`` tail). Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.

get.description:
Poll a job.

Returns ``{job_id, status, created_at}`` plus ``completed_at`` and
``result`` (on ``completed``) or ``error: {code, message}`` (on
``failed``). Statuses: ``pending`` → ``processing`` →
``completed`` | ``failed``.

parameters.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
parameters.name: job_id

schema.description: The identifier of the job to retrieve, as returned by the create-job request.
schema.title: Job Id

Opaque, server-minted, and stable for the life of the job — the same id is returned on the sync response, the async 202, and every poll. Treat it as opaque; older id formats remain accepted indefinitely and are never re-issued.
